    Running Analysis Pipelines

    03_running-pipelines.Rmd

    Overview

    utilrep2 organises the correlates_reporting2 pipeline into three analysis families, each with an individual module function and a full pipeline orchestrator:

    Family Pipeline function Report function
    Immunogenicity run_immuno_analysis() run_immuno_report()
    Correlates of risk (CoR) run_cor_analysis() run_cor_report()
    Correlates of protection (CoP) run_cop_analysis() run_cop_report()

    Each pipeline function calls its constituent module runners in the correct order, mirroring the root Makefile targets (immuno_analysis, cor_analysis, cop_analysis).

    Session setup

    platform and data_path must be set before any run_*() function will execute. Call setup_session() at the start of every session:

    library(utilrep2)

# Linux / macOS
setup_session(
  platform    = "linux",
  data_path   = "/data/projects/correlates_reporting2/data_clean",
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  report_root = "/data/projects/correlates_reporting2"
)

# Windows — win/ scripts co-located with the project (default)
setup_session(
  platform    = "win",
  data_path   = "C:/projects/correlates_reporting2/data_clean",
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  report_root = "C:/projects/correlates_reporting2"
)

# Windows — win/ scripts on a separate drive or network share
setup_session(
  platform    = "win",
  data_path   = "C:/projects/correlates_reporting2/data_clean",
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  report_root = "C:/projects/correlates_reporting2",
  win_root    = "D:/win_scripts"
)

    To use the mock data from the project’s data_clean/ folder:

    setup_session(
  platform    = "linux",
  data_path   = file.path("/path/to/correlates_reporting2", "data_clean"),
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  report_root = "/path/to/correlates_reporting2"
)

    See vignette("getting-started", package = "utilrep2") for full details on each setting and session_status() to verify the current configuration.

    Immunogenicity

    Step 1 – Tabular summary

    Generates tables of biomarker distributions, geometric mean titres, and response rates.

    run_immuno_tabular(trial = "moderna_mock")

    Step 2 – Graphical summary

    Produces violin plots, two-phase sampling plots, and demographic stratification figures.

    run_immuno_graphical(trial = "moderna_mock")

    Full pipeline

    Runs both steps above in sequence:

    run_immuno_analysis(trial = "moderna_mock")

    keep_going = TRUE (the default) mirrors Make’s -k flag: a failure in immuno_tabular is reported but does not abort immuno_graphical.

    Run analysis and build the PDF in one call

    run_immuno_report() runs the full analysis pipeline (run_immuno_analysis()) and then assembles the multi-chapter bookdown PDF via bash _build.sh immuno:

    run_immuno_report(trial = "moderna_mock")

    Use this when you want everything in a single call. If you have already run run_immuno_analysis() and only want to (re-)build the PDF, call build_chapter_report() directly instead (see Building a chapter PDF directly).

    Windows note: The _build.sh assembly step is skipped on Windows. The analysis still runs; to produce the PDF, run _build.sh immuno manually under WSL or Git Bash.

    Correlates of Risk (CoR)

    Individual modules

    Function Equivalent Makefile target
    run_cor_tabular() cd cor_tabular && make
    run_cor_coxph() cd cor_coxph && make
    run_cor_graphical() cd cor_graphical && make
    run_cor_nonlinear() cd cor_nonlinear && make
    run_cor_threshold() cd cor_threshold && make
    run_cor_npthreshold() cd cor_npthreshold && make
    run_cor_logistic() cd cor_logistic && make
    run_cor_poisson() cd cor_poisson && make
    run_cor_surrogates() cd cor_surrogates && make all

    All accept trial, clean, report, and use_win arguments. Run any module in isolation:

    run_cor_coxph(
  trial  = "moderna_mock",
  clean  = TRUE,   # delete output/<trial> first
  report = TRUE    # build chapter PDF afterwards
)

    Full CoR pipeline 

    run_cor_analysis(trial = "moderna_mock")

    Runs tabular → coxph → graphical → nonlinear, keeping going on failures.

    Run analysis and build the PDF in one call

    run_cor_report() runs run_cor_analysis() then calls bash _build.sh cor:

    run_cor_report(trial = "moderna_mock")

    If the analysis is already complete and you only want to rebuild the PDF, use build_chapter_report() for individual chapters instead.

    Trial-specific notes

    Several modules dispatch different scripts depending on the TRIAL label. Unsupported trials emit an informational message and return TRUE without running any scripts.

    # Stage-aware dispatch (vat08_combined)
Sys.setenv(stage = "2")
run_cor_coxph(trial = "vat08_combined")

    Correlates of Protection (CoP)

    Individual modules

    Function Module
    run_cop_contrisk() cop_contrisk
    run_cop_stochastic() cop_stochastic
    run_cop_mediation() cop_mediation
    run_cop_binaryprinstrat() cop_binaryprinstrat 
    run_cop_contrisk(trial = "nextgen_mock")

    run_cop_stochastic() supports all trials that have a completed CoR analysis:

    run_cop_stochastic(trial = "moderna_mock")

    run_cop_mediation() accepts an additional stage argument for vat08_combined:

    run_cop_mediation(trial = "vat08_combined", stage = 2L)

    Supported trials

    Trial run_cop_contrisk() run_cop_stochastic() run_cop_mediation() run_cop_binaryprinstrat()
    nextgen_mock ✔
    moderna_mock / moderna_real ✔ ✔
    janssen_pooled_mock / *_EUA ✔ ✔
    hvtn705 / hvtn705V1V2 ✔ ✔
    vat08_combined ✔ ✔
    nextgen_mock (mediation) ✔

    Full CoP pipeline 

    run_cop_analysis(trial = "vat08_combined")

    Run analysis and build the PDF in one call

    run_cop_report() runs run_cop_analysis() then calls bash _build.sh cop:

    run_cop_report(trial = "vat08_combined")

    Utility functions

    Cleaning module output

    Remove stale output for the current trial before a fresh run:

    clean_module("cor_coxph", trial = "moderna_mock")

    extra_globs accepts additional glob patterns relative to the module root:

    clean_module(
  "cor_graphical",
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  extra_globs = c("data_clean/*.rds", "figs/*.pdf")
)

    Styling source files

    Re-format all R and Rmd source files in a module with styler:

    style_module("cor_coxph")    # one module
style_all()                  # all known modules

    Building a chapter PDF directly 

    build_chapter_report("cor_coxph", trial = "moderna_mock")

    Common patterns

    Restore settings after a block

    setup_session() returns the previous values of every argument it changed, so you can restore them afterwards. Note that platform and data_path must already be set from an earlier call — the snippet below only overrides trial, platform, and win_root:

    old <- setup_session(
  trial    = "janssen_pooled_partA",
  platform = "win",
  win_root = "D:/win_scripts"     # optional: override win/ location
)
on.exit(do.call(setup_session, old), add = TRUE)

run_cor_analysis()

    Redirect output without touching the source tree 

    old_out <- set_output_root("/results/correlates/2026-03-04")
on.exit(set_output_root(old_out), add = TRUE)

run_immuno_analysis(trial = "moderna_mock")

    Skip the report build during development

    All run_*() functions accept report = FALSE to skip the PDF build and iterate faster:

    run_cor_coxph(trial = "moderna_mock", report = FALSE)

    Developed by Y H.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
